commit.message:
fix(model-3d): fix face splitting — snap line to plane + use triangle lookup

Three fixes for face splitting:
1. Snap line endpoints onto face plane before 2D projection (fixes
   floating-point errors from matrixWorld.invert() causing missed
   intersections)
2. Use triangleToHEFace for O(1) face lookup instead of findFaceAtPoint
   (avoids point-in-polygon edge cases at vertices/edges)
3. Include updatedAt in edge geometry key so edges re-render after splits

commit.message:
Update brand color to #078084

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Letterboxd watchlist import (#137)

* feat: add Letterboxd watchlist scraper + matching library

Implements the core backend for the Letterboxd import feature:

- scrapeLetterboxdWatchlist(): Cheerio-based scraper with pagination,
  rate limiting (1s between pages), 500-entry cap, and structured
  error handling (user_not_found, private_watchlist, empty_watchlist,
  rate_limited, network_error)

- matchAndEnrich(): Matches Letterboxd entries against the local film
  DB using normalized title comparison with year +/-1 tolerance, then
  batch-queries upcoming screenings to build EnrichedFilm results with
  next-screening info and isLastChance flag

- getOrCreateImportResults(): In-memory cached (1hr TTL) orchestrator
  that ties scraping and matching together

All queries use Drizzle ORM helpers (gte, eq, inArray) to avoid the
Date serialization gotcha with raw sql template literals.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add cache eviction, username validation, and contentType filter to letterboxd-import

- Add MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES (50) and evict expired/oldest entries
- Validate username format in scraper (defense-in-depth)
- Filter films by contentType='film' to skip events/live broadcasts
- Export normalizeTitle for unit testing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add Letterboxd import API routes (preview + authenticated import)

Add two API routes for the Letterboxd watchlist import feature:

- POST /api/letterboxd/preview (unauthenticated): validates username,
  calls getOrCreateImportResults with caching, maps LetterboxdImportError
  codes to HTTP statuses (404/403/422/429/500), returns matched films
  with pendingLookup count.

- POST /api/user/import-letterboxd (authenticated): accepts filmIds array
  (max 500), looks up denormalized film metadata from films table, batch
  upserts into userFilmStatuses with status "want_to_see" using
  onConflictDoUpdate on the (userId, filmId) unique constraint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add Trigger.dev background task for Letterboxd import TMDB lookup

When users import a Letterboxd watchlist, entries that don't match any
existing film in our database are now sent to a background Trigger.dev
task that looks them up via TMDB, creates film records, and adds them
to the user's watchlist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add Letterboxd import UI components for watchlist

Add the import flow UI with 4-state progression (idle -> scraping ->
results/error). Users enter a Letterboxd username, see matched films
with screening data, and can save them to their watchlist. Includes
PostHog analytics, accessibility (aria-live, focus management), and
Clerk auth integration for save CTA.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: split monolithic store.ts into Zustand slice pattern (#18)

Break down 1246-line store.ts into 14 focused modules:
- store/helpers.ts: shared utilities (persistBody, removeBody, genId, now, workflowDefaults, appendEvent)
- store/seeds.ts: seed data (categories, folders, tags, notes)
- store/types.ts: PlotState interface
- store/migrate.ts: v11→v17 migration logic
- store/selectors.ts: getFilteredNotes, filterNotesByRoute, getFilterTitle, getViewTitle
- store/slices/notes.ts: CRUD + pin/archive/chain
- store/slices/workflow.ts: triage + SRS actions
- store/slices/folders.ts: folder CRUD
- store/slices/tags.ts: tag CRUD + note tagging
- store/slices/categories.ts: category CRUD
- store/slices/thinking.ts: thinking chains + wiki links
- store/slices/maps.ts: knowledge maps + body hydration
- store/slices/ui.ts: sidebar, search, view state, details panel
- store/index.ts: compose all slices, re-export public API

All existing imports work unchanged (resolves to store/index.ts).
Zero type errors, 113 tests pass, build succeeds.

fix: guard actual_state in set-hardware-component-state for older distros

Some versions of controller_manager_msgs do not include actual_state in
the SetHardwareComponentState response, causing an AttributeError. Use
hasattr() to conditionally include it so the command works across all
supported ROS 2 distros.

feat: add pipeline and orchestrate for multi-agent collaboration

Pipeline: chain agents sequentially — output of one feeds into the next.
  zeptopm pipeline "researcher,writer" "Explain quantum computing"

Orchestrate: manager agent delegates to other agents using @delegate()
markers, results are fed back, and manager produces a final answer.
  zeptopm orchestrate manager "Research and write a blog post"

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(pfuq): wire 1-2-3 protocol correctly in follow-up consent handler

- YES bucket now detects residual content ("yes but I also want to know...").
  Pure YES fires booking handoff (tier 1). YES with extra content falls
  through to LLMAgent (tier 2) which receives full input + PFUQ context.
- NO bucket now tries LLMAgent first (tier 2) before falling back to
  canned response (tier 3), consistent with REPROMPT/HESITANT/COMPLEX.

PFUQ consent flow is now a true 1-2-3:
  1. Pure YES → deterministic booking handoff
  2. Everything else → LLMAgent with follow-up question context
  3. Fallback → canned bucket responses if LLMAgent disabled/fails

fix: handle bare /start in Telegram bot (no deep link param)

When Telegram drops the deep link parameter on re-visits, the bot

commit.message:
feat: dietary tag backfill, auto-approve toggle, extraction speed tuning

- Add POST /api/corrections/backfill-dietary-tags: cursor-based batch
  processing of all recipes (500 at a time), heuristic ingredient
  analysis for 9 dietary flags (pork-free, gluten-free, lactose-free,
  vegetarian, vegan, high-protein, low-carb, mediterranean, low-fat).
  Only upgrades false→true, never downgrades manual edits.

- Add autoApprove field to Cookbook model (both Prisma schemas + types).
  When enabled, extraction forces all recipes to "approved" status
  regardless of confidence score. Toggle added to UploadPage options.

- Tune extraction speed: BATCH_SIZE 5→7, add 1000+ page tier (batch 12),
  MAX_CONCURRENT_OPENAI_CALLS 30→35.

commit.message:
feat: add cellAvailable + bookableForMenu to slots API and booking UI

Slots API now returns two availability fields per slot:
- cellAvailable: grid cell has capacity (interval-based overlap)
- bookableForMenu: selected menu duration fits without conflict

This fixes the UX gap where a slot showed ○ (available) but /reserve
would fail because the menu's duration overlapped a neighboring
reservation.

Changes:
- Workers /slots: checkCapacity() helper computes both interval-based
  and duration-based availability; response includes cellAvailable +
  bookableForMenu fields; backward compatible (available = cellAvailable)
- bookingApi.ts: TimeSlot interface gains cellAvailable/bookableForMenu
- StepDatetime.tsx: three-level display (○ bookable / △ cell open but
  menu too long / × full); only bookableForMenu slots are selectable
- StepConfirm.tsx: pre-flight slot check uses bookableForMenu

Workers deployed: version 5141a226
Verified: 06:00 cellAvailable=true bookableForMenu=false with 60min
menu on 45min grid (adjacent 06:45 reservation blocks duration fit)
